description_long: This is a freestanding wooden shelving unit with a rustic, crate-like design, featuring three horizontal shelves divided vertically into two columns. The front and sides are constructed with vertical slats, creating open gaps that allow visibility and airflow, while the back is more enclosed but still has vertical slots. The shelves are flat and sturdy, suitable for storing or displaying various items, and the legs are short and blocky, elevating the unit slightly off the ground. The wood grain texture is visible throughout, giving the piece a natural, unfinished appearance. There are no doors, handles, or glass panels, emphasizing its open-access, utilitarian function.
description: A rustic, open wooden shelving unit with three shelves and vertical slats, divided into two columns. The design is crate-like, with visible wood grain and no doors or handles.
description_view_0: Front view showing three open shelves divided into two columns by vertical slats, with clear visibility into each compartment.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the depth of the unit and the vertical slats, with open gaps between them.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a more enclosed back with vertical slots, providing partial visibility and ventilation.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the vertical slats and open structure.
